note: Catalog entries are NOT deep-researched records. They exist so that every job is findable. Pay, retirement, transition and civilian-crosswalk data are deliberately absent rather than invented. ASVAB line scores are drawn from a secondary aggregator (operationmilitarykids.org) cross-referenced against Wikipedia's List of United States Army careers and are not independently confirmed against a primary DA Pam 611-21 document this session; treat as directional. Officer and warrant officer coverage is representative (one entry per main basic branch / warrant career field) rather than exhaustive of every functional-area sub-specialty code, which run into the hundreds and are not part of this catalog's scope.

specialties.name: Infantryman
specialties.what_it_is: Engages the enemy on foot using small arms and anti-armor weapons as part of a rifle squad.

specialties.name: Indirect Fire Infantryman (Mortarman)
specialties.what_it_is: Operates and employs mortar systems to provide indirect fire support to infantry units.

specialties.name: Combat Engineer
specialties.what_it_is: Builds and clears obstacles, constructs fighting positions, and conducts route/mine clearance in support of maneuver forces.

specialties.name: Bridge Crewmember
specialties.what_it_is: Constructs and emplaces tactical bridging to support unit river-crossing and gap-crossing operations.

specialties.name: U.S. Army Diver
specialties.what_it_is: Performs underwater construction, salvage, and demolition tasks as a combat engineer diver.

specialties.name: Quarrying Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Operates rock-crushing and quarrying equipment to produce aggregate for construction projects.

specialties.name: Plumber
specialties.what_it_is: Installs and repairs water, sewage, and gas piping systems on Army facilities.

specialties.name: Firefighter
specialties.what_it_is: Responds to structural, aircraft, and wildland fires and performs rescue operations on Army installations.

specialties.name: Horizontal Construction Engineer
specialties.what_it_is: Operates heavy equipment such as bulldozers and graders to build and maintain roads, airfields, and earthworks.

specialties.name: Prime Power Production Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Operates and maintains large-scale tactical and commercial electrical power-generation systems.

specialties.name: Power Line Distribution Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Installs and maintains electrical power distribution lines and substations.

specialties.name: Interior Electrician
specialties.what_it_is: Installs and maintains interior electrical wiring and fixtures in Army buildings.

specialties.name: Technical Engineer Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Performs engineer surveying, drafting, and construction-materials testing to support engineer planning.

specialties.name: Concrete and Asphalt Equipment Operator
specialties.what_it_is: Operates paving, mixing, and concrete-placement equipment for construction projects.

specialties.name: Carpentry and Masonry Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Builds and repairs wood-frame and masonry structures.

specialties.name: Geospatial Engineer
specialties.what_it_is: Produces maps, terrain visualizations, and geospatial data products to support mission planning.

specialties.name: Cannon Crewmember
specialties.what_it_is: Loads, aims, and fires towed and self-propelled cannon artillery systems.

specialties.name: Fire Support Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Requests, adjusts, and coordinates indirect fire support for maneuver units.

specialties.name: Fire Control Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Operates fire direction center equipment to compute firing data for artillery units.

specialties.name: Multiple Launch Rocket System (MLRS/HIMARS) Crewmember
specialties.what_it_is: Operates and maintains rocket artillery launcher systems.

specialties.name: Field Artillery Firefinder Radar Operator
specialties.what_it_is: Operates counterfire target-acquisition radar systems to locate enemy indirect-fire weapons.

specialties.name: Field Artillery Surveyor/Meteorological Crewmember
specialties.what_it_is: Provides survey and meteorological data used to compute artillery firing solutions.
specialties.source: en.wikipedia.org (List of United States Army careers)

specialties.name: Patriot Fire Control Enhanced Operator/Maintainer
specialties.what_it_is: Operates and maintains the fire control system of the Patriot air-defense missile system.

specialties.name: Air Defense Battle Management System Operator
specialties.what_it_is: Operates command-and-control systems that manage air and missile defense engagements.

specialties.name: Air Defense Enhanced Early Warning System Operator
specialties.what_it_is: Operates radar and sensor systems that provide early warning of aerial and missile threats.

specialties.name: Air and Missile Defense Crewmember
specialties.what_it_is: Operates short-range air defense weapon systems to counter aerial threats.

specialties.name: PATRIOT Launching Station Enhanced Operator/Maintainer
specialties.what_it_is: Operates and maintains Patriot missile launching stations.

specialties.name: Avenger Crewmember
specialties.what_it_is: Operates the Avenger short-range air defense weapon system.
specialties.source: en.wikipedia.org (List of United States Army careers)

specialties.name: Aircraft Powerplant Repairer
specialties.what_it_is: Inspects, maintains, and repairs aircraft engines.

specialties.name: Aircraft Powertrain Repairer
specialties.what_it_is: Inspects, maintains, and repairs helicopter transmissions and drive systems.

specialties.name: Unmanned Aircraft Systems Repairer
specialties.what_it_is: Maintains and repairs Army unmanned aircraft systems and their ground support equipment.

specialties.name: Aircraft Electrician
specialties.what_it_is: Maintains and repairs aircraft electrical systems.

specialties.name: Aircraft Structural Repairer
specialties.what_it_is: Repairs aircraft airframes, sheet metal, and structural components.

specialties.name: Aircraft Pneudraulics Repairer
specialties.what_it_is: Maintains and repairs aircraft hydraulic and pneumatic systems.

specialties.name: Aircraft Components Repair Supervisor
specialties.what_it_is: Supervises shop-level repair of aircraft components.
specialties.source: en.wikipedia.org (List of United States Army careers)

specialties.name: Avionic Mechanic
specialties.what_it_is: Maintains and repairs aircraft avionics and electronic navigation systems.

specialties.name: Air Traffic Control Operator
specialties.what_it_is: Directs the movement of military aircraft in terminal and en-route airspace.

specialties.name: AH-64 Attack Helicopter Repairer
specialties.what_it_is: Maintains and repairs AH-64 Apache attack helicopters.

specialties.name: UH-60 Helicopter Repairer
specialties.what_it_is: Maintains and repairs UH-60 Black Hawk helicopters.

specialties.name: CH-47 Helicopter Repairer
specialties.what_it_is: Maintains and repairs CH-47 Chinook cargo helicopters.

specialties.name: Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) Operator
specialties.what_it_is: Operates unmanned aerial systems to conduct reconnaissance and surveillance missions.

specialties.name: AH-64D Armament/Electrical/Avionic Systems Repairer
specialties.what_it_is: Maintains armament, electrical, and avionics systems on AH-64D helicopters.

specialties.name: Aviation Operations Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Coordinates flight scheduling, flight plans, and airspace clearances for Army aviation units.

specialties.name: Cyber Operations Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Conducts offensive and defensive cyberspace operations to protect Army networks and target adversary systems.

specialties.name: Electronic Warfare Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Plans and executes electronic warfare operations to disrupt or exploit enemy use of the electromagnetic spectrum.
specialties.source: en.wikipedia.org (List of United States Army careers)

specialties.name: Special Forces Candidate
specialties.what_it_is: Enlistment option/pipeline for training and selection into a Special Forces enlisted specialty (18B/C/D/E/F).

specialties.name: Special Forces Weapons Sergeant
specialties.what_it_is: Trains and leads foreign and U.S. forces on a wide range of light and heavy weapons systems on a Special Forces team.
specialties.source: en.wikipedia.org (List of United States Army careers)

specialties.name: Special Forces Engineer Sergeant
specialties.what_it_is: Performs combat engineering, demolitions, and construction tasks on a Special Forces team.
specialties.source: en.wikipedia.org (List of United States Army careers)

specialties.name: Special Forces Medical Sergeant
specialties.what_it_is: Provides advanced trauma and medical care to a Special Forces team and partner-nation forces.
specialties.source: en.wikipedia.org (List of United States Army careers)

specialties.name: Special Forces Communications Sergeant
specialties.what_it_is: Installs, operates, and maintains communications systems for a Special Forces team.
specialties.source: en.wikipedia.org (List of United States Army careers)

specialties.name: Special Forces Intelligence Sergeant
specialties.what_it_is: Collects, analyzes, and disseminates intelligence for a Special Forces team's operations.
specialties.source: en.wikipedia.org (List of United States Army careers)

specialties.name: Cavalry Scout
specialties.what_it_is: Conducts reconnaissance and security operations for ground maneuver forces.

specialties.name: M1 Armor Crewman
specialties.what_it_is: Operates and fights as a crew member on the M1 Abrams main battle tank.

specialties.name: Information Technology Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Installs, operates, and maintains computer systems and local/wide-area networks.

specialties.name: Radio Operator-Maintainer
specialties.what_it_is: Operates and performs maintenance on tactical radio communications equipment.

specialties.name: Cyber Network Defender
specialties.what_it_is: Monitors and defends Army networks against cyber intrusions.

specialties.name: Network Communication Systems Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Installs and maintains network communications systems and equipment.
specialties.source: en.wikipedia.org (List of United States Army careers)

specialties.name: Cable Systems Installer/Maintainer
specialties.what_it_is: Installs and maintains fiber-optic and copper cable communications systems.

specialties.name: Nodal Network Systems Operator-Maintainer
specialties.what_it_is: Operates and maintains nodal network switching systems that route tactical communications traffic.

specialties.name: Microwave Systems Operator/Maintainer
specialties.what_it_is: Operates and maintains line-of-sight microwave transmission systems.

specialties.name: Multichannel Transmission Systems Operator-Maintainer
specialties.what_it_is: Operates and maintains multichannel satellite and terrestrial transmission systems.

specialties.name: Satellite Communications Systems Operator/Maintainer
specialties.what_it_is: Operates and maintains satellite communications terminals.

specialties.name: Signal Support Systems Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Installs, operates, and performs unit-level maintenance on tactical communications and computer systems.

specialties.name: Combat Documentation/Production Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Records and produces combat photography, video, and multimedia documentation.
specialties.source: en.wikipedia.org (List of United States Army careers)

specialties.name: Paralegal Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Provides legal administrative and paralegal support to Army judge advocates.

specialties.name: Military Police
specialties.what_it_is: Enforces military law and order, conducts law-enforcement patrols, and provides physical security.

specialties.name: Criminal Investigation Division (CID) Special Agent
specialties.what_it_is: Conducts criminal investigations of felony-level offenses involving Army personnel or property.

specialties.name: Internment/Resettlement Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Operates military corrections and detention facilities and manages detainee/internee operations.

specialties.name: Military Working Dog Handler
specialties.what_it_is: Trains and handles military working dogs for patrol, detection, and security missions.

specialties.name: Interpreter/Translator
specialties.what_it_is: Provides foreign-language translation and interpretation support to Army units.
specialties.source: en.wikipedia.org (List of United States Army careers)

specialties.name: Intelligence Analyst
specialties.what_it_is: Analyzes and synthesizes intelligence from multiple sources to support commanders' decision-making.

specialties.name: Geospatial Intelligence Imagery Analyst
specialties.what_it_is: Analyzes imagery and geospatial data to produce intelligence products.

specialties.name: Counterintelligence Special Agent
specialties.what_it_is: Conducts investigations and operations to detect and neutralize foreign intelligence threats.

specialties.name: Human Intelligence Collector
specialties.what_it_is: Conducts interrogations, debriefings, and source operations to collect human intelligence.

specialties.name: Signals Intelligence Analyst
specialties.what_it_is: Analyzes intercepted signals to produce intelligence reports.

specialties.name: Cryptologic Linguist
specialties.what_it_is: Translates and analyzes foreign-language communications in support of signals intelligence operations.

specialties.name: Cryptologic Cyberspace Intelligence Collector/Analyst
specialties.what_it_is: Collects and analyzes intelligence related to cyberspace and cryptologic operations.

specialties.name: Signals Collector/Analyst
specialties.what_it_is: Operates signals collection systems and analyzes intercepted communications.

specialties.name: Military Intelligence Systems Maintainer/Integrator
specialties.what_it_is: Installs, maintains, and integrates military intelligence systems and equipment.

specialties.name: Foreign Language Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Applies foreign-language proficiency to intelligence collection and analysis tasks.
specialties.source: en.wikipedia.org (List of United States Army careers)

specialties.name: Financial Management Technician
specialties.what_it_is: Processes military pay, travel, and disbursing transactions for units.

specialties.name: Psychological Operations Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Develops and disseminates persuasive information products to influence foreign target audiences.

specialties.name: Civil Affairs Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Assesses and coordinates with local governments and civilian populations in military operations areas.

specialties.name: Tactical Space Operations Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Supports operational space, high-altitude, and missile-defense missions; the Army's first enlisted space-specific MOS, effective October 1, 2026.

specialties.name: Human Resources Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Provides personnel management, records, and administrative support to soldiers and units.

specialties.name: Musician
specialties.what_it_is: Performs as an instrumentalist in an Army band; accession requires a competitive audition.
specialties.source: en.wikipedia.org (List of United States Army careers)

specialties.name: Special Band Member
specialties.what_it_is: Performs in a special-designation Army band (e.g., ceremonial or premier bands); accession requires audition.
specialties.source: en.wikipedia.org (List of United States Army careers)

specialties.name: Talent Acquisition Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Serves as an Army recruiter, identifying and enlisting qualified applicants.
specialties.source: en.wikipedia.org (List of United States Army careers)

specialties.name: Public Affairs Mass Communications Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Writes, edits, and produces news and information content about Army activities for public release.

specialties.name: Visual Information Equipment Operator-Maintainer
specialties.what_it_is: Operates and maintains visual information production and broadcast equipment.

specialties.name: Visual Information Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Produces photography, video, and graphic products documenting Army operations and activities.

specialties.name: Religious Affairs Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Provides administrative and force-protection support to Army chaplains and religious programs.

specialties.name: Biomedical Equipment Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Inspects, maintains, and repairs medical equipment and devices.

specialties.name: Orthopedic Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Assists with the diagnosis and treatment of musculoskeletal injuries and conditions.

specialties.name: Practical Nursing Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Provides direct patient nursing care under the supervision of physicians and nurses.

specialties.name: Operating Room Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Assists surgeons and prepares operating rooms for surgical procedures.

specialties.name: Dental Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Assists Army dentists with clinical procedures and maintains dental treatment facilities.

specialties.name: Physical Therapy Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Assists physical therapists in rehabilitating soldiers with injuries.

specialties.name: Patient Administration Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Manages patient medical records and administrative processing at Army medical facilities.

specialties.name: Optical Laboratory Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Fabricates and repairs eyeglasses and optical devices.

specialties.name: Medical Logistics Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Manages the acquisition, storage, and distribution of medical supplies and equipment.

specialties.name: Medical Laboratory Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Performs laboratory tests on tissue, blood, and other specimens to support diagnosis.

specialties.name: Occupational Therapy Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Assists occupational therapists in rehabilitating soldiers to perform daily and duty-related tasks.

specialties.name: Nutrition Care Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Plans therapeutic diets and manages food service operations in Army medical treatment facilities.

specialties.name: Radiology Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Operates radiographic imaging equipment to produce diagnostic images.

specialties.name: Pharmacy Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Prepares and dispenses medications under the supervision of a pharmacist.

specialties.name: Veterinary Food Inspection Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Inspects food supplies and provides animal-care support under veterinary supervision.

specialties.name: Preventive Medicine Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Conducts sanitation, pest-control, and disease-prevention inspections and programs.

specialties.name: Animal Care Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Provides veterinary technician support for military working animals.

specialties.name: Respiratory Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Provides respiratory therapy and diagnostic testing to patients.

specialties.name: Combat Medic Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Provides emergency medical treatment and evacuation support in field and combat environments.

specialties.name: Behavioral Health Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Assists behavioral health providers in treating soldiers with mental health conditions.

specialties.name: Eye Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Assists ophthalmologists and optometrists with eye examinations and minor procedures.

specialties.name: Chemical, Biological, Radiological and Nuclear (CBRN) Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Detects, identifies, and decontaminates chemical, biological, radiological, and nuclear hazards.

specialties.name: Cargo Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Loads, unloads, and documents cargo moved by air, rail, highway, and water.

specialties.name: Watercraft Operator
specialties.what_it_is: Operates Army watercraft used for logistics-over-the-shore and inland waterway transport.

specialties.name: Watercraft Engineer
specialties.what_it_is: Operates and maintains propulsion and mechanical systems aboard Army watercraft.

specialties.name: Motor Transport Operator
specialties.what_it_is: Operates wheeled vehicles to transport personnel and cargo.

specialties.name: Transportation Management Coordinator
specialties.what_it_is: Plans and coordinates the movement of personnel and cargo across transportation modes.

specialties.name: Railway Section Repairer
specialties.what_it_is: Maintains and repairs military railway track and roadbed (Reserve Component).

specialties.name: Railway Operations Crewmember
specialties.what_it_is: Operates military rail equipment and coordinates rail movements (Reserve Component).

specialties.name: Ammunition Stock Control and Accounting Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Manages accountability, records, and stock control of ammunition supplies.

specialties.name: Ammunition Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Receives, stores, issues, and inspects conventional ammunition and explosives.

specialties.name: Explosive Ordnance Disposal Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Locates, identifies, renders safe, and disposes of explosive ordnance and hazardous devices.

specialties.name: M1 Abrams Tank System Maintainer
specialties.what_it_is: Maintains and repairs M1 Abrams tank systems.

specialties.name: Wheeled Vehicle Mechanic
specialties.what_it_is: Performs maintenance and recovery on wheeled military vehicles.

specialties.name: Utilities Equipment Repairer
specialties.what_it_is: Maintains and repairs heating, cooling, and utility equipment.

specialties.name: Tactical Power Generation Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Maintains and repairs tactical power-generation equipment.

specialties.name: Allied Trade Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Performs welding, machining, and metalworking repairs to support equipment maintenance.

specialties.name: Small Arms/Artillery Repairer
specialties.what_it_is: Repairs and maintains small arms and towed artillery weapon systems.

specialties.name: Fire Control Repairer
specialties.what_it_is: Maintains and repairs fire control instruments and optical systems on combat vehicles.

specialties.name: Track Vehicle Mechanic
specialties.what_it_is: Maintains and repairs tracked combat vehicles other than tanks and Bradleys.

specialties.name: Quartermaster and Chemical Equipment Repairer
specialties.what_it_is: Maintains and repairs quartermaster and chemical support equipment.

specialties.name: Construction Equipment Repairer
specialties.what_it_is: Maintains and repairs heavy construction equipment.

specialties.name: Bradley Fighting Vehicle System Maintainer
specialties.what_it_is: Maintains and repairs Bradley Fighting Vehicle systems.

specialties.name: Artillery Mechanic
specialties.what_it_is: Maintains and repairs self-propelled artillery systems.

specialties.name: Stryker Systems Maintainer
specialties.what_it_is: Maintains and repairs Stryker wheeled combat vehicle systems.

specialties.name: Automated Logistical Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Manages supply records, requisitions, and warehouse operations using automated logistics systems.

specialties.name: Petroleum Supply Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Receives, stores, tests, and issues bulk and packaged petroleum products.
specialties.source: en.wikipedia.org (List of United States Army careers)

specialties.name: Culinary Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Prepares and serves food in dining facilities and field kitchens.

specialties.name: Petroleum Laboratory Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Tests fuel and petroleum products for quality and contamination.

specialties.name: Mortuary Affairs Specialist

specialties.name: Parachute Rigger

specialties.name: Shower, Laundry and Clothing Repair Specialist

specialties.name: Water Treatment Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Operates and maintains water purification and treatment equipment.

specialties.name: Unit Supply Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Manages unit-level supply operations, property accountability, and requisitions.

specialties.name: Land Combat Electronic Missile System Repairer
specialties.what_it_is: Repairs electronic components of land combat missile systems.

specialties.name: Air Traffic Control Equipment Repairer
specialties.what_it_is: Maintains and repairs air traffic control radar and communications equipment.
specialties.source: en.wikipedia.org (List of United States Army careers)

specialties.name: Radio and Communications Security (COMSEC) Repairer
specialties.what_it_is: Maintains and repairs radio and communications security equipment.
specialties.source: en.wikipedia.org (List of United States Army careers)

specialties.name: Computer/Detection Systems Repairer
specialties.what_it_is: Maintains and repairs computer and detection systems.
specialties.source: en.wikipedia.org (List of United States Army careers)

specialties.name: Test, Measurement, and Diagnostic Equipment Maintenance Support Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Calibrates and repairs test, measurement, and diagnostic equipment.
specialties.source: en.wikipedia.org (List of United States Army careers)

specialties.name: Radar Repairer
specialties.what_it_is: Maintains and repairs radar systems.
specialties.source: en.wikipedia.org (List of United States Army careers)

specialties.name: Avionic and Survivability Equipment Repairer
specialties.what_it_is: Maintains and repairs aircraft avionics and survivability equipment.
specialties.source: en.wikipedia.org (List of United States Army careers)

specialties.name: PATRIOT System Repairer
specialties.what_it_is: Maintains and repairs Patriot missile system electronics.
specialties.source: en.wikipedia.org (List of United States Army careers)

specialties.name: AVENGER System Repairer
specialties.what_it_is: Maintains and repairs Avenger air defense system electronics.
specialties.source: en.wikipedia.org (List of United States Army careers)

specialties.name: Integrated Family of Test Equipment Operator/Maintainer
specialties.what_it_is: Operates and maintains standardized electronic test equipment used across maintenance units.
specialties.source: en.wikipedia.org (List of United States Army careers)

specialties.name: Recruiter
specialties.what_it_is: Recruits qualified applicants for enlistment in the Army.
specialties.source: en.wikipedia.org (List of United States Army careers)

specialties.name: Career Counselor
specialties.what_it_is: Advises soldiers on reenlistment options and manages unit retention programs.
specialties.source: en.wikipedia.org (List of United States Army careers)

specialties.name: Infantry Officer
specialties.what_it_is: Leads infantry units in ground combat operations.
specialties.source: en.wikipedia.org (List of United States Army careers)

specialties.name: Engineer Officer
specialties.what_it_is: Leads engineer units performing construction, mobility, and countermobility missions.
specialties.source: en.wikipedia.org (List of United States Army careers)

specialties.name: Field Artillery Officer
specialties.what_it_is: Leads field artillery units and plans and coordinates indirect fires.
specialties.source: en.wikipedia.org (List of United States Army careers)

specialties.name: Air Defense Artillery Officer
specialties.what_it_is: Leads air and missile defense units.
specialties.source: en.wikipedia.org (List of United States Army careers)

specialties.name: Aviation Officer
specialties.what_it_is: Serves as an Army aviator or aviation unit leader.
specialties.source: en.wikipedia.org (List of United States Army careers)

specialties.name: Cyber Warfare Officer
specialties.what_it_is: Leads offensive and defensive cyberspace operations units.

specialties.name: Special Forces Officer
specialties.what_it_is: Leads Special Forces operational detachments.
specialties.source: en.wikipedia.org (List of United States Army careers)

specialties.name: Armor Officer
specialties.what_it_is: Leads armor and cavalry units.
specialties.source: en.wikipedia.org (List of United States Army careers)

specialties.name: Signal Officer
specialties.what_it_is: Leads units that plan, install, and operate Army communications networks.

specialties.name: Judge Advocate

specialties.name: Military Police Officer
specialties.what_it_is: Leads military police units performing law enforcement, security, and detention operations.

specialties.name: Military Intelligence Officer
specialties.what_it_is: Leads intelligence units and manages the intelligence collection and analysis process.

specialties.name: Financial Manager
specialties.what_it_is: Manages unit-level and command-level financial management and resourcing operations.

specialties.name: Psychological Operations Officer
specialties.what_it_is: Leads units that plan and execute psychological operations.

specialties.name: Civil Affairs Officer
specialties.what_it_is: Leads units that coordinate between military forces and civilian authorities/populations.
specialties.source: en.wikipedia.org (List of United States Army careers)

specialties.name: Space Operations Officer
specialties.what_it_is: Plans and executes Army space-support operations (functional area officer branch).
specialties.source: en.wikipedia.org (List of United States Army careers)

specialties.name: Human Resources Officer
specialties.what_it_is: Leads personnel and human resources support operations.
specialties.source: en.wikipedia.org (List of United States Army careers)

specialties.name: Army Bands Officer
specialties.what_it_is: Commands and directs an Army band.
specialties.source: en.wikipedia.org (List of United States Army careers)

specialties.name: Public Affairs Officer
specialties.what_it_is: Leads public affairs operations and serves as a unit spokesperson.
specialties.source: en.wikipedia.org (List of United States Army careers)

specialties.name: Chaplain
specialties.what_it_is: Provides religious support and pastoral counseling to soldiers and families; ordained clergy commissioned as a noncombatant officer.
specialties.source: en.wikipedia.org (List of United States Army careers)

specialties.name: Veterinary Corps Officer
specialties.what_it_is: Provides veterinary care for military working animals and food-safety inspection.
specialties.source: en.wikipedia.org (List of United States Army careers)

specialties.name: Physical Therapist
specialties.what_it_is: Provides physical therapy evaluation and treatment as a Medical Specialist Corps officer.
specialties.source: en.wikipedia.org (List of United States Army careers)

specialties.name: Health Services (Medical Service Corps)
specialties.what_it_is: Manages administrative and operational functions of Army medical units.
specialties.source: en.wikipedia.org (List of United States Army careers)

specialties.name: Chemical Officer
specialties.what_it_is: Leads CBRN reconnaissance, decontamination, and hazard-response units.
specialties.source: en.wikipedia.org (List of United States Army careers)

specialties.name: Transportation Officer
specialties.what_it_is: Leads units that move personnel and cargo by ground, rail, air, and water.

sources.title: List of United States Army careers
Army MOS List: All Current Enlisted Jobs in 2026 (Over 140 MOS) - Operation Military Kids
US Army MOS List - mosdb.com (consulted, but excluded from this catalog as primary source because it mixes current and long-obsolete Cold War-era MOS codes without clear currency labeling)
Army Jobs / Military Occupational Specialties (MOS) List - Army-Portal.com
Army seeks Soldiers to become space operations specialists
Army announces new Space Operations Branch
18X Special Forces Recruit - Army ASVAB Requirements - Cogn-IQ
Army COOL - Select an MOC (attempted; JavaScript-rendered, no static MOS list retrievable)
Explore All Army Jobs & Careers - goarmy.com (attempted via browser-header curl; JavaScript-rendered SPA shell, no static job list retrievable)

unverified: ASVAB line-score figures throughout this catalog are sourced from operationmilitarykids.org, a secondary aggregator, not independently confirmed against a primary DA Pam 611-21 or goarmy.com per-MOS page this session. Treat as directional pending primary-source confirmation.

Security clearance level was left null for nearly all entries because no primary per-MOS source was fetched this session confirming the specific level; only 17C (Top Secret/SCI), 31B (Secret), 89D (Secret), and 40D (Top Secret) carried session-sourced clearance claims, and even those are drawn from secondary/official-summary sources rather than a fetched primary MOS fact sheet.

MOS 40D (Tactical Space Operations Specialist) is confirmed via official army.mil reporting as the Army's first enlisted space-operations MOS, but per that same reporting it does not become effective until October 1, 2026 (after this catalog's 2026-07-12 retrieval date) and its accession pipeline was still in an application phase (Jan-Apr 2026) at time of writing. This directly updates (and should be reconciled with) this project's own data/interest-taxonomy.json, whose space-satellite cluster notes state Army has 'no organic enlisted space-operations career field' -- that note predates this development.

A naming/coding conflict was found between sources for the Aviation Operations Specialist role: this project's own data/interest-taxonomy.json cites '93P', while a Wikipedia-sourced fetch this session and some secondary sites (military-ranks.org, armyreal.com) cite '15P' for the same title. This catalog uses 93P for consistency with the project's existing taxonomy file, but the discrepancy is unresolved and should be checked against a primary source.

Officer MOS codes 60A-62B (Medical Corps), 63A-63R (Dental Corps), and 66B-66W (Army Nurse Corps) were reported by the Wikipedia source only as ranges covering multiple specialties, not as a single canonical accession code; this catalog marks their 'code' field as UNVERIFIED rather than guessing a specific single code.

Several enlisted codes (25H Network Communication Systems Specialist, 94D/94E/94F/94H/94M/94R/94S/94T/94Y electronic-maintenance codes, 92F Petroleum Supply Specialist, 35W Foreign Language Specialist, 15K Aircraft Components Repair Supervisor, 79R/79S recruiting codes, 92R/13T/14S/17E/18B-18F/25V) appear in the Wikipedia source but were not cross-confirmed in the operationmilitarykids.org ASVAB-focused source this session; included because Wikipedia's list is clearly organized as current (modern branch/corps names, no Cold War-era legacy systems), but flagged as single-source within this session.

Generic branch-level senior NCO/CSM 'consolidator' designators (e.g., 12Z, 13Z, 14Z, 15Z, 18Z, 25X/25Y/25Z, 35X, 68Z, 88Z, 91X/91Z, 92Z, 94Z, 97Z) were deliberately EXCLUDED from this catalog -- they represent rank-based redesignation within a career management field at the senior-NCO level rather than a distinct job a person chooses, so including them would not add real findability value.

Officer and warrant officer coverage is intentionally representative, not exhaustive: real Army officer functional-area and warrant-officer sub-specialty code lists run into the hundreds (e.g., every Medical Corps sub-specialty, every aviation warrant track 151A-155G, every academy-professor code 47A-47U). Only one illustrative entry per main basic branch / main warrant career field is included per this task's instruction to cover 'the main warrant/officer branches.'

mosdb.com was fetched and reviewed but excluded as a direct source for this catalog's specialty list because its 'Army MOS' page appears to aggregate the entire historical MOS numbering system going back to the Cold War (e.g., Hawk, Nike, Vulcan, Chaparral, M48/M60 tank-series codes that are long since retired) without a clear currency marker, creating fabrication risk if used uncritically.
completion_accountability: 206 of an estimated ~190-200 total Army MOS/branch entries catalogued this session (158 enlisted MOS, 32 representative officer basic-branch entries, 16 representative warrant-officer branch entries). Confidence: MODERATE-HIGH on enlisted MOS completeness -- cross-referenced two independently structured 2026-dated sources (Wikipedia's List of United States Army careers and operationmilitarykids.org) that agreed on the large majority of current codes and both excluded obvious Cold War-era legacy systems found in a third source (mosdb.com) that was deliberately not used as ground truth. Gaps acknowledged: (1) exact current membership of a handful of niche/Reserve-Component-only 12-series and 88-series codes was not independently verified against DA Pam 611-21 or a live goarmy.com job page, since goarmy.com's browse-jobs page and Army COOL's MOS index are both JavaScript-rendered single-page apps that returned no static job data even with full browser headers; (2) officer and warrant officer coverage is deliberately representative (one entry per main branch), not exhaustive of every functional-area/sub-specialty code, per the task's 'main warrant/officer branches' framing; (3) ASVAB line scores and ASVAB-only ('CO', 'GT', 'ST', etc.) values throughout are secondary-sourced (operationmilitarykids.org), not confirmed against a primary Army document.
